description_long: This is a classic, upright fire hydrant with a domed top and a cylindrical body, predominantly painted in a bright red color with contrasting silver-gray caps and fittings. The hydrant features three hose connection outlets: two on the sides and one larger outlet facing the front, each secured with a cap and attached chains. The top of the hydrant has a hexagonal operating nut, used for opening the valve to release water. The base is flanged and bolted, designed for secure installation into the ground. The surface is smooth and glossy, with visible bolts and minor surface wear, typical of hydrants found on city streets. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A red, domed fire hydrant with silver-gray caps, chains, and a flanged base, featuring three hose outlets and a glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large central outlet, two side outlets, all with caps and chains, and the domed top with the operating nut.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the left outlet and its chain, as well as the flanged base and bolts.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant, both side outlets, and the absence of a rear outlet.
description_view_3: Angled side view from the right, showing the right outlet, its cap and chain, and the domed top.
